title: caragana decorticans var. decorticans
intro: Caragana decorticans var. decorticans, also known as Siberian peashrub, is a shrub native to Central Asia and Eastern Europe, characterized by its thorny branches and clusters of yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Caragana decorticans var. decorticans include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in the relief of cold and flu symptoms. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be dried and used in capsules or as a powder for easier consumption.
